assertions.text: Subject uses 'pg_dump:' lowercase prefix + colon
Subject is imperative mood, no trailing period, capitalized
No conventional-commits prefix
Body is paragraph prose (not bullet diff-recap)
Discussion: uses postgr.es/m/ shortener (not www.postgresql.org/message-id)
No Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer
No Signed-off-by trailer
No Backpatch-through line (master-only)
Author: line OMITTED when committer is sole author (new in iter-2)
Explicitly rejects Co-Authored-By: Claude for upstream PG
Distinguishes lowercase Co-authored-by: vs uppercase Co-Authored-By:
States attribution credits humans only
Points to Author:/Reviewed-by:/Reported-by: as correct trailers
Notes names/emails are verbatim from From: header
States PG is NOT a DCO project / no Signed-off-by
Mentions omit Author: when committer is sole author (new in iter-2)
Backpatch-through value is the OLDEST branch (16)
Format is bare major version number (16), not '16-18' or 'REL_16_STABLE'
Commit message is IDENTICAL across all branches
Mentions release-note tooling rationale
Diagnosed-by named as real PG trailer
Reviewers one-per-line, NOT comma-separated
Backpatch-through goes last in trailer block
Explicitly shows GOOD vs BAD format examples (new in iter-2)

notes: Iter-2 added 3 new precision assertions (A9 on E1, A7 on E2, A8 on E3) corresponding to the 3 applied skill edits. All pass with_skill, all fail baseline.
Iter-1 → iter-2 baseline: 4/21 → 4/24 (no improvement, expected — baseline did not change).
Skill is now at ceiling for these evals.
